task_completion.description: The DICOM scan from the specified directory is loaded into Blue Sky Plan 5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blue Sky Plan 5 application window is open and displays dental CBCT images (e.g., axial, cross-sectional, panoramic, or 3D views).

task_completion.description: Vertical bone height and buccolingual width measurements are made for the lower right posterior jaw sites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Measurement lines with distance values (typically in mm) are visible on the cross-sectional or panoramic views of the lower right jaw, indicating height (ridge crest to nerve canal) and width.

task_completion.description: Annotations are added to document the assessment of the sites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text annotations, notes, or labels are visible on the scan views or within an annotations/notes panel in the Blue Sky Plan interface.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as site_assessment.bsp in the specified BlueSkyPlanTasks directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project' dialog showing the path C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BlueSkyPlanTasks\site_assessment.bsp, the application title bar showing the filename, or the file visible in the File Explorer.

task_completion.description: At least two cross-sectional images are exported to the site_images directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog saving images to the C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\BlueSkyPlanTasks\site_images\ directory, or the File Explorer showing at least two image files in that folder.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Blue Sky Plan 5 GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with Blue Sky Plan's menus, measurement tools, and export dialogs, rather than terminal commands generating files.

integrity.description: Measurements and annotations are created using the software's built-in tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement lines and text annotations match the native UI style of Blue Sky Plan 5 and are placed correctly within the DICOM viewing panes, not drawn over the screen with an external image editor.

integrity.description: The agent used Blue Sky Plan 5 as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface clearly belongs to Blue Sky Plan 5, identifiable by its layout, toolbars, and window title.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Blue Sky Plan 5 to assess a dental CBCT scan. The lower right posterior jaw corresponds to teeth #44 (second premolar), #46 (first molar), and #47 (second molar) in the FDI numbering system. Measurements must include vertical bone height (from alveolar ridge crest to the inferior alveolar nerve canal) and buccolingual width (buccal to lingual bone width). The project must be saved as 'site_assessment.bsp' and at least 2 cross-sectional images must be exported to the 'site_images' folder.
